summary: DHH shares his unexpectedly enthusiastic experience owning a Tesla Model S Plaid, comparing it favorably against his extensive collection of exotic supercars. Despite costing a fraction of his Lamborghinis and Ferraris, the Plaid's 1,000+ horsepower electric drivetrain delivers acceleration that surpasses them all while remaining perfectly controllable. He argues the car's handling, practicality, and software make it far more than a straight-line speed trick. The post frames the Plaid as democratizing progress — exotic-level performance becoming accessible to ordinary buyers.
key_points: The Tesla Model S Plaid delivers over 1,000 horsepower for $86K, matching the thrill of cars costing five to ten times more
EV traction control makes massive power safe and approachable in ways combustion engines never could, eliminating the scary loss-of-grip moments common in fast gasoline cars
The real joy of horsepower is acceleration, not top speed — every stoplight and onramp becomes an opportunity
The Plaid handles at 90% the level of a Porsche Taycan and is surprisingly livable as a daily driver
Exotic performance is being democratized: today's $86K car will be a $40K used car in a few years
money_quotes: The pleasure of power isn't about top speed, it's about acceleration, and I have never, ever owned a car that accelerates like the Model S Plaid in these intervals.
It's intoxicating and addictive, in the best, most wholesome possible way.

An old school gasoline-powered car can feel scary with as little as 300-400 horsepower. Traction breaks, you need quick hands to catch it. That's the frame of reference anyone who've driven a fast, regular car would have. But it doesn't apply at all in the EV world.
Very few people will ever end up owning a great Ferrari V12 or a Porsche V10. Lots and lots of people will either own or get a ride in Tesla Plaid. That's progress!
It's the kind of democratizing progress that Warhol talks about when he quips that the billionaire and the bum both have access to the best soft drink in the world: A Coca Cola.
I expected a one-trick pony. But this is so much more.
Even if you're an incarnate petrol head, you owe yourself a ride in a Plaid, and I will almost guarantee that you'll feel better about the future.
key_insight: The Tesla Model S Plaid represents a democratization of exotic automotive performance, delivering thrills that once required half-million-dollar supercars at a fraction of the price and with far greater accessibility and safety.
